A value-returning provider coexists with routes on the same struct type.
-- go.mod --
module app

go 1.26
-- main.go --
package main

import "os"

func main() { os.Exit(run()) }
-- web/web.go --
package web

import "net/http"

type API struct{}

//fabrik:provider
func NewAPI() API { return API{} }

type Consumer struct {
	A API
}

//fabrik:http GET /consumer
func (c *Consumer) Get(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}

//fabrik:http GET /api
func (a *API) Get(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {}
-- want/main.gen.go --
// Code generated by fabrik. DO NOT EDIT.
// Regenerate with: fabrik wire

package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"os"

	"github.com/gofabrik/fabrik/router"

	"app/web"
)

func run() int {
	if err := func() error {
		// Providers
		webAPI := web.NewAPI()
		webConsumer := &web.Consumer{
			A: webAPI,
		}

		r := router.New()
		webAPI2 := &web.API{}

		// Register
		r.Method("GET", "/consumer", webConsumer.Get)
		r.Method("GET", "/api", webAPI2.Get)
		return nil
	}(); err != nil {
		fmt.Fprintln(os.Stderr, "app:", err)
		return 1
	}
	return 0
}
